It's just vegetable oil, not worth it to spend what name brands cost. I will say, they have managed to make the packaging SO THIN they practically need to put it in a cardboard over box like they do for restaurants. SO thin that if you are not real careful to NOT SQUEEZE all the air out when you are screwing the lid on, it will get so tight, like a vacuum in the bottle, it will be super hard to UNSCREW the lid.

Wish they could bring it back it $1

The actual oil was fine. The top of the bottle was mashed down so badly that i could hardly get the lid off. Did the shopper take it off the shelf like that and just give it to me because i ordered online? Or, did the associate who loaded it sit something heavy like my case of water or other drinks on top of it? I just wish that people who work in customer service cared enough to pay attention.

This vegetable oil is a solid everyday staple. It has a neutral flavor that works well for cooking, baking, and frying without affecting the taste of food. It heats evenly and performs reliably whether I am sautéing, deep frying, or using it in recipes. The bottle size is convenient and lasts a while, and the pour is easy to control without making a mess. For the price, the quality is consistent and dependable. If you want a straightforward, affordable cooking oil that does exactly what it should, this one is a great value.

I haven't gotten this order yet. Originally it should have been here yesterday afternoon. Got notice of delay, okay fine roads are still not good in some places. Then notice of another delay, then another delay putting it after 6p.m., which it would be dark and temperatures dropping so I rescheduled it to today between 10-12. This morning they were preparing order. The order has been delayed again. It is now past 2:30p.m. If they weren't going to deliver why not say so at start. I have items on that order specifically because of the storm coming, so I really need this order. As far as I know everything is in the order, at least it was last night.

I ordered a large gallon-sized jug they was out of stock so I had to settle for the bottles who bottles like this cost me more than the gallon judgment and then I only got one of them even though I was charged for two Plus when I called the customer service agent gave me a $5 off coupon promo only good online orders so anything under a certain amount who's going to cost me more than the $5 and shipping or to pick up not worth The trouble I went through then when I did try to use it but coupon promo it was no good two days later

I ALWAYS BUY GV VEGETABLE OIL. I AM ON A TIGHT BUDGET SO PRICING IS IMPORTANT BUT I STILL WANT QUALITY. I CANT TELL THE DIFF FROM THE MORE EXPENSIVE BRANDS. SOMETIMES I NEED TO GET A SMALL BOTTLE AT DOLLAR GENERAL SO I BUY THE MORE EXPENSIVE BUT ONLY THEN. GREAT PRODUCT!

Basic cooking oil that works for just about anything that requires a high smoke point oil for cooking. The taste doesn't change the flavor of foods. It can also be used for greasing pans for a non-stick surface. Best used in light color pans when going in the oven.

As the home chef for my 7 family members, I cook a lot of food. There is no solid reason to use a brand-name vegetable oil when the Great Value brand vegetable oil is perfectly fine. It has a mild flavor, and I use it when frying any type of foods, when making salad dressings, when baking calls for oil, etc. I'm very pleased with the Great Value Vegetable Oil, and you will be too. Make the switch!

This is oil. Unless you use Olive or Coconut or even Lard, there is not much difference between the vegetable oils. Except price. Oil has gotten outrageous!. Walmart has the best prices on oil and pretty much everything. I must add that I use to only shop Kroger. Walmart beats all other stores on price while keeping up with Quality.

Just as good as the more expensive name brands %26 is the only oil I use. It would be great, though, if they could do a "no-drip" spout as I normally only need a small bit of oil at a time %26 end up having to wipe the top with a paper towel after each use.

I am scared to order my chicken wings now. the $4.44 pack of restaurant wings $4.44 Usually a favorite but all my meat was spoiled that I order including my burgers the white castle burgers

Vegetable oil is of great quality. The texture and consistency taste like a brand name. An excellent option for those of us trying to cut back on ridiculous brand-name, high-priced products. Walmart is better than any other brand, in my opinion. I have used this vegetable oil for baking brownies, cookies, and a specialty vegetable dish, and all came out amazingly good. Don't bother paying more when you can get great quality for less.

This oil has the performance level of the higher end Brands and yet is at the affordable price point that as an everyday pantry Item we stock it and use it light enough to even make an Italian dressing versus olive oil that cost more so if you have to pinch a few pennies but want an oil that will go both ways this is it

Now, I understand all the hoopla about certain types of unhealthy cooking oils... and I too have committed to a healthier lifestyle (yeah, right!). So, there I stood, in the aisle, reading every vegetable oil label... and honestly, Great Value Vegetable Oil was nearly the only brand that is 100% Veg. oil- the healthiest kind. Healthiest and most affordable... I'm in.

This giant jug of Great Value vegetable oil is a crucial component of the college experience, specifically for deep-frying frozen chicken nuggets or attempting to make actual food for the first time. The 48 fl oz size ensures he won't have to panic-buy oil for at least a month or two, which, given his priorities, is a minor miracle. Thanks, Walmart, for providing the necessary fuel for his inevitable culinary disasters.

i love this very affordable product. easy to use with my cooking. The packaging is very durable and safe, and it tastes ok. it won't burn after the first use. i can reuse it at least 2 times. and i will always continue to use and buy this for my cooking for my family. thank you, great value. my families appreciate you

The vegetable oil is run of the mill vegetable oil. It's affordable. I'm here because Walmart cancels your delivery orders without warning and without any attempt to reschedule them. They cancelled mine AGAIN recently which means they hold your money for 10 business days too. That's right. They cancel your order and then HOLD your money. I've called my bank. I can't do anything about it because Walmart controls the transaction. So not only do we have no groceries, but the $200 an additional amount they charge you each time, will be on hold, and unavailable for ten business days. You're better off using Amazon or Doordash. Don't bother, especially if you live in Norfolk, VA and use the Tidewater Drive store. They are particularly bad about this.

I've been buying the Great Value Vegetable Oil for years, and it never disappoints. It's perfect for everyday cooking—whether I'm frying, baking, or sautéing. The oil has a neutral flavor that doesn't overpower food, and it heats evenly. The 48 fl oz size is a great deal for the price and lasts a good while in my kitchen. The bottle is easy to pour and store. Overall, an affordable, reliable staple for any home cook.

The only oil I buy these days I use this in the deep fryer, straight in the skillet, and in recipes. Out of the 5 grocery stores in my area, Walmart continues to be the most affordable.
I used this oil to make doughnuts!! The turned out great!! There was no residual oil taste which is awesome! I would recommend this oil!! I think better than Crisco
It's Vegetable Cooking Oil. Not much else to say, it works as advertised. Light cooking oil in my opinion doesn't give food an off flavor kinda just does its thing like it should.

I use Great Value Vegetable Oil for all my baking and frying. I see no difference in quality or taste from the more expensive name brands. I think the price makes it the best option for vegetable oil.

Great Value basic baking and pantry staples are very good quality, more affordable and a great way to keep items needed for baking, cooking and meals always on hand. The quality is consistent make me a loyal purchaser.
I have bought this brand of vegetable oil for many years. It is a great value for the price. It always tastes fresh. No reason to spend more when this oil is just as great as the expensive brand.
This oil is super light and doesn't leave an after taste. It's good to use when frying, when making salad dressings and for any thing you use vegetable oil in. Great price too.
I used to buy another cornnoul brand but they kept raising the price. Then I gave Walmart's Great Value oil a try and I never looked back. Great cooking oil for a fraction of the usual price.
Good quality oil in a size that works for me. I use quite a bit of oil for baking, this oil is great to have on hand and I am very pleased with it. Highly recommend!
Great Value cooking oil is great for frying or baking. It has a very light taste. Serves the same purpose as the expensive brands, but for a lower price.
I was asked to review this purchase. It's vegetable oil. Not a whole lot to say. If you need vegetable oil and don't buy into the hype to buy name brand, buy this one.
There's no difference between the national brand versus the store brand other than the price. I use this for all my cooking and baking needs. I've never had any issues with this product.
